narrative:
Markets are firmly in a business as usual regime this morning, with a stress score of 14 out of 100 signalling calm conditions across the board. Equity returns are essentially flat on a five-day basis, with a z-score near zero confirming there is no directional momentum of note in either direction.

The VIX at 16.1 sits at the 28th percentile of its ten-year history and the term structure is in normal contango, meaning options markets are pricing no near-term disruption. Credit is fully corroborating that picture: high yield spreads at 274 basis points are at the 10th percentile of their five-year range, and investment grade at 76 basis points carries a z-score of negative 1.07, both indicating that credit is historically tight and offering no warning signal whatsoever.

The sector picture is worth a brief note, with five of eleven sectors in the red over five days and a sharp dispersion between the best and worst performers. Technology leads with a 6.4 percent gain, while consumer discretionary and communication services have both lost roughly four percent, and Brazil via EWZ is the standout laggard at negative 4.1 percent over the period. That divergence between tech strength and consumer-facing weakness is the one cross-current worth monitoring, even if aggregate stress remains negligible.
